title: Rewrite interactive init prompts to use menu-driven runner and stack selection
description: Update `collect_init_answers()` to replace free-text runner and language prompts with numbered menus, derive test/build defaults from the selected stack, and preserve the free-text fallback flow when `Other` is chosen.
acceptance_criteria: Project stack selection uses `_select_from_list()` over `STACKS` display names.
Choosing a predefined stack pre-fills test and build/check defaults from `STACKS` while still allowing overrides through `_prompt()`.
Choosing `Other` falls back to free-text prompts for language, test command, and build/check command.
`InitAnswers.language` stores the canonical display name for predefined stacks.

handoff_summary.completed: All five acceptance criteria met: runner uses `_select_from_list()` with claude/codex; stack uses `_select_from_list()` over STACKS display names; predefined stacks pre-fill defaults with `_prompt()` overrides; `Other` falls back to free-text; `InitAnswers.language` stores canonical display name.
handoff_summary.risks: The `test_onboarding_scaffold.py` input sequences for `test_accepts_defaults`, `test_invalid_max_workers_then_valid`, and `test_negative_max_workers_rejected` are unchanged and pass through cleanly. The two tests that used free-text runner input (`test_custom_values`, `test_invalid_runner_then_valid`) were updated to use numeric inputs compatible with `_select_from_list()`.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent verifies all collect_init_answers test cases, menu validation edge cases (out-of-range, non-integer), and Other-stack free-text path.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Downstream bead B-3722ddd9 (non-interactive defaults using STACKS[0]) has no conflict risk — it touches `cli/commands/init.py`, not `prompts.py`.
handoff_summary.design_decisions:
Runner validation: replaced the `while True` + string comparison loop with a single `_select_from_list()` call. This removes the possibility of typos and eliminates the need for error-message copy inside `collect_init_answers`. The old loop tested `runner in ("claude", "codex")`; the menu can only return a valid index, so the validation concern is fully delegated to `_select_from_list`.

Stack detection for "Other": checked as `selected_stack[0] == "Other"` rather than `stack_idx == len(STACKS) - 1`. This makes the condition self-documenting and tolerates future reordering of `STACKS` without breaking the branch.

Free-text "Other" defaults: `test_command` and `build_check_command` default to `""` (no default), not `"pytest"` / `"python -m py_compile"`. A user choosing "Other" is explicitly outside the catalog, so suggesting Python defaults would be misleading.

Language for predefined stacks: set directly to `selected_stack[0]` (no `_prompt()` call). The display name is the canonical identifier and users cannot override it — this keeps `InitAnswers.language` consistent with `STACKS` and downstream consumers like `_language_specific_known_issues()`.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes:
Key paths for the tester:
1. All-empty input still yields Python defaults (menus default to index 0).
2. Runner menu: non-integer input (e.g. "notanumber") is rejected by `_select_from_list` with an error message and re-prompt.
3. Runner menu: out-of-range integer (e.g. "5") is rejected and re-prompted.
4. Stack menu: selecting each predefined stack populates the correct language, test_command, and build_check_command from STACKS.
5. Stack "Other" (index 6, menu item 7): triggers free-text prompts; user-entered language/test/build are preserved verbatim.
6. Predefined stack with command overrides: user types a non-empty string over the default test/build commands.
7. Max workers validation path is unchanged from before.

conflict_risks: Downstream bead B-3722ddd9 (non-interactive defaults using STACKS[0]) has no conflict risk — it touches `cli/commands/init.py`, not `prompts.py`.

execution_history.summary: Rewrote `collect_init_answers()` in `src/agent_takt/onboarding/prompts.py` to use numbered menus for runner and stack selection.
